executiveSummary.text: Allegion plc (ALLE) is now back in sync with the canonical DYOR HQ sheet after the metadata repair pass. The current stance is REDUCE with conviction 50/100, using $144.81 as the latest stored price reference where available.

businessModel.text: Allegion plc sells security and access-control products, so demand is tied to non-residential construction, retrofit activity and distribution execution. The repaired note now matches the corrected company metadata and removes the broken generic template.

recentCatalysts.text: Commercial building demand, price-cost balance, product mix and retrofit spending are the main near-term drivers. Electronic security adoption can support the medium-term mix.

thesisEvaluation.text: Base case: the core operating backdrop remains good enough to support the present reduce stance, but not strong enough to justify a looser risk view without better evidence. Bull case: execution and demand improve faster than expected. Bear case: operating or funding pressure worsens and compresses valuation further.

keyRisks.text: Construction slowdowns can weigh on volume. Input-cost inflation can pressure margins. Channel inventory swings can distort quarterly results. Competition can limit pricing power. Execution missteps in higher-value electronic offerings can delay rerating.

risks.risk: Construction slowdowns can weigh on volume.
Channel inventory swings can distort quarterly results.
Execution missteps in higher-value electronic offerings can delay rerating.

whoShouldOwn.text: Own: Investors who want exposure to building-security and renovation demand. Avoid: Investors expecting rapid secular growth without cyclical noise.

recommendation.text: REDUCE - Conviction: 50/100. This rebuilt page is intentionally concise, but it is now internally coherent and aligned to the corrected ticker and company metadata.

entryExit.text: Keep position sizing consistent with a reduce stance until fuller company-specific analysis is refreshed.

sources.marketData: Live quote data sourced via the DYOR HQ proprietary market data workflow and internal canonical mapping.
sources.companyFilings: Public company filings, regulatory disclosures and investor materials where available.
